TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == bala' == ËÇ∫ u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. siwkay nu balat u bala' sa, u kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw atu aadupan. anu mihanhan kita, macumuden ku bali tayza i bala', zikuzan sa, tayza tu labu nuizang. sisa mauzip kita. u kakalimaan a kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw. buting, kacumuli...izaw tu ku bala'. u pacucu'ay a aadupan sa , situsa a bala'. pabeli ku bali katukuh i kidu-mahmin. pabeli ku O2,mibalic ku CO2. kyu mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika "ËÇ∫Ê≥°". The lungs are the primary organs of the respiratory system in humans and many other animals including a few fish and some snails. In mammals and most other vertebrates, two lungs are located near the backbone on either side of the heart. Their function in the respiratory system is to extract oxygen from the atmosphere and transfer it into the bloodstream, and to release carbon dioxide from the bloodstream into the atmosphere, in a process of gas exchange. Respiration is driven by different muscular systems in different species. Mammals, reptiles and birds use their different muscles to support and foster breathing. In early tetrapods, air was driven into the lungs by the pharyngeal muscles via buccal pumping, a mechanism still seen in amphibians. In humans, the main muscle of respiration that drives breathing is the diaphragm. The lungs also provide airflow that makes vocal sounds including human speech possible.
S.PHON: == bar~…æa î~ ° == * u a≈ãa≈ãan nu ƒßanƒßan ita ku bar~…æa î~ °. siwkaj nu bar~…æat u bar~…æa î~ ° sa, u kidu-sakar~…æuk nu tad…ômaw atu aadupan. anu miƒßanƒßan kita, ma ¶umud…ôn ku bar~…æi tajza i bar~…æa î~ °, zikuzan sa, tajza tu r~…æabu nuiza≈ã. sisa mauzip kita. u kakar~…æimaan a kidu-sakar~…æuk nu tad…ômaw. buti≈ã, ka ¶umur~…æi...izaw tu ku bar~…æa î~ °. u pa ¶u ¶u î~ °aj a aadupan sa , situsa a bar~…æa î~ °. pab…ôr~…æi ku bar~…æi katukuƒß i kidu-maƒßmin. pab…ôr~…æi ku o*,mibar~…æi ¶ ku ¶o*. kju mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u a≈ãa≈ãan nu ƒßanƒßan ita ku bar~…æa î~ °. u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika "**". tƒß…ô r~…æu≈ãs ar…ô tƒß…ô primarj organs of tƒß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm in ƒßumans and manj otƒß…ôr animar~…æs in ¶r~…æudi≈ã a f…ôw fisƒß and som…ô snair~…æs. in mammar~…æs and most otƒß…ôr *…ôrt…ôbrat…ôs, two r~…æu≈ãs ar…ô r~…æo ¶at…ôd n…ôar tƒß…ô ba ¶kbon…ô on …ôitƒß…ôr sid…ô of tƒß…ô ƒß…ôart. tƒß…ôir fun ¶tion in tƒß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm is to …ô*tra ¶t o*jg…ôn from tƒß…ô atmospƒß…ôr…ô and transf…ôr it into tƒß…ô br~…æoodstr…ôam, and to r…ôr~…æ…ôas…ô ¶arbon dio*id…ô from tƒß…ô br~…æoodstr…ôam into tƒß…ô atmospƒß…ôr…ô, in a pro ¶…ôss of gas …ô* ¶ƒßa≈ã…ô. *…ôspiration is dri*…ôn bj diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶ur~…æar sjst…ôms in diff…ôr…ônt sp…ô ¶i…ôs. mammar~…æs, r…ôptir~…æ…ôs and birds us…ô tƒß…ôir diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶r~…æ…ôs to support and fost…ôr br…ôatƒßi≈ã. in …ôarr~…æj t…ôtrapods, air was dri*…ôn into tƒß…ô r~…æu≈ãs bj tƒß…ô pƒßarj≈ã…ôar~…æ mus ¶r~…æ…ôs *ia bu ¶ ¶ar~…æ pumpi≈ã, a m…ô ¶ƒßanism stir~…ær~…æ s…ô…ôn in ampƒßibians. in ƒßumans, tƒß…ô main mus ¶r~…æ…ô of r…ôspiration tƒßat dri*…ôs br…ôatƒßi≈ã is tƒß…ô diapƒßragm. tƒß…ô r~…æu≈ãs ar~…æso pro*id…ô airfr~…æow tƒßat mak…ôs *o ¶ar~…æ sounds in ¶r~…æudi≈ã ƒßuman sp…ô…ô ¶ƒß possibr~…æ…ô.
S.FORM: == bala' == ËÇ∫ u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. siwkay nu balat u bala' sa, u kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw atu aadupan. anu mihanhan kita, macumuden ku bali tayza i bala', zikuzan sa, tayza tu labu nuizang. sisa mauzip kita. u kakalimaan a kidu-sakaluk nu tademaw. buting, kacumuli...izaw tu ku bala'. u pacucu'ay a aadupan sa , situsa a bala'. pabeli ku bali katukuh i kidu-mahmin. pabeli ku O2,mibalic ku CO2. kyu mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u angangan nu hanhan ita ku bala'. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika "ËÇ∫Ê≥°". The lungs are the primary organs of the respiratory system in humans and many other animals including a few fish and some snails. In mammals and most other vertebrates, two lungs are located near the backbone on either side of the heart. Their function in the respiratory system is to extract oxygen from the atmosphere and transfer it into the bloodstream, and to release carbon dioxide from the bloodstream into the atmosphere, in a process of gas exchange. Respiration is driven by different muscular systems in different species. Mammals, reptiles and birds use their different muscles to support and foster breathing. In early tetrapods, air was driven into the lungs by the pharyngeal muscles via buccal pumping, a mechanism still seen in amphibians. In humans, the main muscle of respiration that drives breathing is the diaphragm. The lungs also provide airflow that makes vocal sounds including human speech possible.
S.PHON: == bar~…æa î~ ° == * u a≈ãa≈ãan nu ƒßanƒßan ita ku bar~…æa î~ °. siwkaj nu bar~…æat u bar~…æa î~ ° sa, u kidu-sakar~…æuk nu tad…ômaw atu aadupan. anu miƒßanƒßan kita, ma ¶umud…ôn ku bar~…æi tajza i bar~…æa î~ °, zikuzan sa, tajza tu r~…æabu nuiza≈ã. sisa mauzip kita. u kakar~…æimaan a kidu-sakar~…æuk nu tad…ômaw. buti≈ã, ka ¶umur~…æi...izaw tu ku bar~…æa î~ °. u pa ¶u ¶u î~ °aj a aadupan sa , situsa a bar~…æa î~ °. pab…ôr~…æi ku bar~…æi katukuƒß i kidu-maƒßmin. pab…ôr~…æi ku o*,mibar~…æi ¶ ku ¶o*. kju mauzip kita. tinaku a kamu u a≈ãa≈ãan nu ƒßanƒßan ita ku bar~…æa î~ °. u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika "**". tƒß…ô r~…æu≈ãs ar…ô tƒß…ô primarj organs of tƒß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm in ƒßumans and manj otƒß…ôr animar~…æs in ¶r~…æudi≈ã a f…ôw fisƒß and som…ô snair~…æs. in mammar~…æs and most otƒß…ôr *…ôrt…ôbrat…ôs, two r~…æu≈ãs ar…ô r~…æo ¶at…ôd n…ôar tƒß…ô ba ¶kbon…ô on …ôitƒß…ôr sid…ô of tƒß…ô ƒß…ôart. tƒß…ôir fun ¶tion in tƒß…ô r…ôspiratorj sjst…ôm is to …ô*tra ¶t o*jg…ôn from tƒß…ô atmospƒß…ôr…ô and transf…ôr it into tƒß…ô br~…æoodstr…ôam, and to r…ôr~…æ…ôas…ô ¶arbon dio*id…ô from tƒß…ô br~…æoodstr…ôam into tƒß…ô atmospƒß…ôr…ô, in a pro ¶…ôss of gas …ô* ¶ƒßa≈ã…ô. *…ôspiration is dri*…ôn bj diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶ur~…æar sjst…ôms in diff…ôr…ônt sp…ô ¶i…ôs. mammar~…æs, r…ôptir~…æ…ôs and birds us…ô tƒß…ôir diff…ôr…ônt mus ¶r~…æ…ôs to support and fost…ôr br…ôatƒßi≈ã. in …ôarr~…æj t…ôtrapods, air was dri*…ôn into tƒß…ô r~…æu≈ãs bj tƒß…ô pƒßarj≈ã…ôar~…æ mus ¶r~…æ…ôs *ia bu ¶ ¶ar~…æ pumpi≈ã, a m…ô ¶ƒßanism stir~…ær~…æ s…ô…ôn in ampƒßibians. in ƒßumans, tƒß…ô main mus ¶r~…æ…ô of r…ôspiration tƒßat dri*…ôs br…ôatƒßi≈ã is tƒß…ô diapƒßragm. tƒß…ô r~…æu≈ãs ar~…æso pro*id…ô airfr~…æow tƒßat mak…ôs *o ¶ar~…æ sounds in ¶r~…æudi≈ã ƒßuman sp…ô…ô ¶ƒß possibr~…æ…ô.
